1.about: The family counselling and conversations on organ donation is one of the most poignant and emotionally intensive conversations that one could ever have. Supporting a family grappling with the devastating loss of a loved one is paramount. The death of the person has to be communicated clearly. Offering a family the option of organ donation needs to be done with sensitivity and empathy. All families need to be offered this option. Thereafter, it is up to the family to make a decision that is right for them.
objectives: To discuss grief counselling and family approach for organ donation
To demonstrate good approach for organ donation
To explain the legal aspects of deceased organ donation
1.whoCanJoin: Candidates aiming to become Transplant coordinators or Counsellors in Intensive Care Units Doctor Nurse Allied Health Science Social Work Psychology Social Science Sociology Public Health Hospital Administration Graduate in any subject

2.about: Understanding the legal framework governing organ donation and transplantation is essential for all professionals involved in this program. Hospitals planning to initiate these programs must be well acquainted with the legal provisions required to secure licenses for donation and transplantation. All stakeholders, including medical professionals, administrators, regulatory authorities, and state, regional, and national organizations, should be aware of and understand the components of the legal framework. MOHAN Foundation offers this short-term online course (20 hours) to equip learners with a comprehensive understanding of the legal aspects of organ donation and transplantation in India. The course is designed to educate all stakeholders involved in donation and transplant programs on the key provisions of the Transplantation of Human Organs Act, the associated Rules, and subsequent amendments. The course additionally includes an overview of transplant laws from a few other nations.
objectives: Understand the legal framework for organ donation and transplantation in India.
Enable participants to navigate the Transplantation of Human Organs Act, Rules, and amendments with confidence.
Prepare hospitals and institutions to meet statutory requirements for licensing and program initiation.
Strengthen understanding of stakeholder roles across clinical, administrative, and regulatory domains.
Provide an overview of transplant laws followed in select other countries.
2.whoCanJoin: Doctors/ medical practitioners Non-medical/ para medical and administrative staff in hospitals Other interested stakeholders (like NGOs, law students etc.)

3.about: MOHAN Foundation offers a “Transplant Coordination Professional Certificate” course for health care professionals. This short-term E-learning course has been designed exclusively for candidates with hospital experience who want to pursue Transplant Coordination. The E-learning modules offer participants flexibility in learning. They can access the course at a time and pace that is convenient to them during the week. In addition, they will get to experience weekend webinars with lectures by guest speakers on key topics, review of study material, interactive sessions, and counselling role plays. It is learning that offers the “Best of Both Worlds.”
objectives: To equip health care professionals to handle the key areas in transplant coordination.
3.whoCanJoin: Graduates in any subject who wish to have a value-add to their qualification Doctor Nurse Allied Health Science Social Work Psychology Social Science Sociology Public Health Hospital Administration Graduate in any subject

5.about: Donation after brainstem death (DBD) remains the main source of organs in deceased organ donation program, both globally and in India. Despite the presence of a legal framework and established national guidelines, the identification and certification of brainstem death remain inconsistent across intensive care units (ICUs) in India. Limited familiarity with certification protocols, and lack of structured training often result in missed opportunities for deceased organ donation in the country. Another major challenge lies in the optimal medical management of a deceased donor following the declaration of brainstem death. Maintaining physiological stability in the ICU to preserve organ function until retrieval requires specialised knowledge, coordinated teamwork, and adherence to standard donor management protocols. Gaps in training and experience often lead to suboptimal donor maintenance, adversely affecting organ utilisation and outcomes. MOHAN Foundation offers this short-term online course (20 hours) for healthcare professionals on ‘Brainstem Death’ aiming at sensitising the ICU doctors and nurses on identifying and certifying brainstem death and donor optimisation. The course is formatted to educate the medical and paramedical professionals with the key aspects of brainstem death and deceased organ donation in India.
objectives: Provide structured knowledge on the determination and certification of brainstem death.
Familiarise participants with the legal and regulatory framework governing deceased organ donation in India.
Orient medical and paramedical professionals to the processes involved in facilitating deceased organ donation.
5.whoCanJoin: ICU professional Doctor Nurse Transplant Coordinator Paramedical professional Anyone who would like to understand the concept of brainstem death and the legal framework in India

6.about: This short-term course is designed to offer medical professionals an opportunity to upskill their knowledge on organ donation and transplantation, thereby enhancing their attitude towards this critical area of healthcare.
objectives: To upskill medical professionals in the principles and practices of organ donation and transplantation
To promote ethical, legal, and compassionate approaches in organ donation and transplant care
To enhance competence in clinical coordination and donor family engagement

7.about: This short-term course is designed to offer paramedical professionals an opportunity to upskill their knowledge on organ donation and transplantation, thereby enhancing their attitude towards this critical area of healthcare.
objectives: To enhance knowledge of organ donation and transplantation among paramedical professionals
To develop a clear understanding of the legal, and ethical aspects of organ donation and transplantation
To foster a positive and proactive attitude towards organ donation
To strengthen skills in supporting donor identification, coordination, and transplant-related processes
To build capacity in coordination and family support roles

8.about: This online, accredited short-term course is designed for healthcare professionals and individuals interested in organ donation and transplantation. It provides a structured overview of the organ transplantation ecosystem, enabling participants to gain both conceptual clarity and practical insights into the science, policy, and coordination involved in the field. Through ten thoughtfully designed modules, learners will explore the entire continuum — from organ failure prevention and donation processes to transplant science, immunology, and ethical-legal frameworks in India.
objectives: By the end of this course, participants will be able to: • Understand the fundamentals of organ donation and transplantation • Describe the legal and ethical framework governing organ donation and transplantation in India • Explain the concept of brainstem death and its certification process • Outline the steps involved in deceased organ donation and coordination • Recognize end-stage organ failure and organ-specific clinical considerations • Appreciate strategies for the prevention of organ failure • Comprehend the basics of transplant immunology • Learn the principles of immunosuppression and rejection management • Explore the emerging role of cell-free DNA (cfDNA) in transplant monitoring • Explore the emerging role of AI in Healthcare & in Organ Transplantation like Kidney Transplantation
8.whoCanJoin: This course is ideal for: • Healthcare professionals — doctors, nurses, coordinators, and allied health workers seeking to strengthen their knowledge base • Marketing professionals who interact with transplant clinicians • Students and trainees in medicine, nursing, or life sciences interested in transplantation science • Volunteers and professionals associated with organ donation programs or NGOs

9.about: MOHAN Foundation offers a one-day online certificate course on Organ Donation ‘Gift of Life’.
objectives: Introducing basic concepts of organ donation and transplantation to improve the knowledge and attitude of general public towards organ donation and transplantation.
9.whoCanJoin: Anyone who is interested in learning more about organ donation and would like help patients suffering from end stage organ failure by spreading the noble cause.

10.about: ‘Taking care of your Kidneys’ is a course that empowers you with validated information about your kidneys. Kidneys are a pair of miracle organs that filter out the waste and toxic material from our body in the form of urine. It is estimated that about 10% of the world’s population is affected by chronic kidney disease (CKD), and the majority are discovered when it is too late to reverse the damage. This course will help you not only make healthy choices but also train you on how to recognise the early symptoms and signs of kidney disease. This course is a must for anyone who has a relative with kidney disease or if they are prone to chronic kidney disease.

11.about: ‘Taking care of your Liver’ is a course that empowers you with validated information about the liver. Liver is an essential and the largest internal organ in the human body. It is considered to be a chemical factory of the human body as it carries out over 500 functions including metabolism, detoxification, immunity, storage and many other. The liver also has a unique capacity to regenerate upon various injuries. This course will help you not only to make healthy choices to maintain liver wellness but also train you on how to recognise the early symptoms and signs of liver disease. This course is a must for anyone who has a relative with liver disease or if they are prone to chronic liver disease.

12.about: The course "Taking Care of Your Lungs" provides you with reliable knowledge on lungs. Lungs are important organs without which no person can survive. Lung disease is a very prevalent but often not prioritised as highly as a lot of other problems. According to the World Health Organization (WHO), chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is the third most common cause of mortality worldwide. This course will help you learn how to recognise the early signs and symptoms of lung disease in addition to assisting you in making healthy choices to maintain lung fitness. Anyone who has a relative with a lung condition or who is at risk for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease must take this course (COPD).
